ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for pulut hitam cake; A0CKP (rice-based sweet snacks and cakes) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for pulut hitam cake or black glutinous rice cake as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of Pulut Hitam Cake weighs approximately 120 g, representing a standard dessert portion as served at a café or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Black glutinous rice cake / kueh estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo component data: black glutinous rice (cooked), coconut cream, sugar, eggs, and wheat flour
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Foodo / Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exists for pulut hitam cake as a composite commercial product. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition: approximately 35% cooked black glutinous rice, 25% coconut cream, 20% sugar, 10% eggs, 10% wheat flour, consistent with traditional pulut hitam kueh recipes. HPB Foodo entries used: 'Black glutinous rice, cooked' (energy ~130 kcal/100g, carbohydrate ~28g, protein ~2.8g, fat ~0.5g), 'Coconut cream' (energy ~197 kcal/100g, fat ~21g, saturated fat ~18.5g), 'Sugar, white' (energy ~387 kcal/100g, carbohydrate ~100g), 'Egg, whole, raw' (energy ~143 kcal/100g, protein ~12.6g, fat ~9.5g), 'Wheat flour, plain' (energy ~364 kcal/100g, carbohydrate ~76g, protein ~10g). Composite estimate for the finished cake product per 100g is derived from these proportions. Values are cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for coconut cream (FDC ID 170172) and cooked glutinous rice (FDC ID 168878) for secondary validation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of black glutinous rice and analogous rice-based dessert products; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Black glutinous rice GI approximately 55 (low); coconut cream addition moderates GI further.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pulut hitam cake as a composite commercial product. GI of 55 is estimated based on: (1) black glutinous rice GI reported as approximately 55 in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables; (2) the moderating effect of coconut cream fat content on glycemic response; (3) the sugar content which partially elevates GI offset by fat and fibre. The composite GI of 55 is a conservative analogous estimate consistent with similar coconut-cream-enriched glutinous rice desserts. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 46.2) / 100 = 25.4, where carbohydrate per 120g serving = 38.5 × 1.2 = 46.2g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~55 mg/100g); low overall sodium load per slice
hypertension.guidance: A single 120g slice contributes approximately 66 mg sodium, well within the recommended limit of 2000 mg/day for hypertensive individuals, making occasional consumption acceptable provided overall dietary sodium is managed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate content (38.5g/100g) and significant sugar content (18g/100g) with a glycemic load of approximately 25 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 25 per 120g slice and high sugar content, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of Pulut Hitam Cake to small portions infrequently, and pair with low-GI foods or protein to blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (~7.8g/100g) from coconut cream, contributing approximately 9.4g saturated fat per 120g slice
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content from coconut cream (approximately 9.4g per slice) exceeds one-third of the recommended daily saturated fat limit for cardiovascular disease patients; individuals with CVD should consume this dessert only occasionally and in small portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pulut Hitam Cake (Sliced) is a Singaporean dessert cake made from black glutinous rice (pulut hitam), coconut cream, sugar, eggs, and wheat flour, assembled as a layered cake and topped with a black rice compote. The cake features a soft, fluffy sponge base infused with black glutinous rice, layered with smooth, rich coconut cream, and finished with a compote of cooked black rice that provides a slightly chewy texture and earthy, nutty flavour. The overall taste profile is mildly sweet with a pronounced coconut richness and the characteristic nuttiness of black glutinous rice. Culturally, pulut hitam is a beloved traditional Malay and Singaporean dessert typically served as a warm porridge with coconut milk; this cake format is a modern café-style interpretation that preserves the traditional flavour profile in a sliceable, plated form. Nutritionally, a 120g slice provides approximately 298 kcal, 46g carbohydrate, 11.4g fat (predominantly saturated from coconut cream), and 4.6g protein. The high saturated fat content from coconut cream and the significant sugar and carbohydrate load make this a high-concern dessert for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low-medium boundary), but the glycemic load per serving is approximately 25, which is clinically significant. Individuals with diabetes or cardiovascular conditions should consume this dessert only occasionally and in small portions. The black glutinous rice provides some dietary fibre and anthocyanins, which have antioxidant properties, offering modest nutritional benefit relative to the overall energy density of the product.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for pulut hitam cake as a composite commercial product. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo component data for black glutinous rice (cooked), coconut cream, sugar, eggs, and wheat flour, weighted by typical recipe proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on black glutinous rice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and cross-referenced, but direct measurement data for this specific commercial product is not available.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo component entries for black glutinous rice (cooked), coconut cream, sugar, eggs, and wheat flour using approximate recipe proportions (35% cooked black glutinous rice, 25% coconut cream, 20% sugar, 10% eggs, 10% wheat flour). These proportions are based on typical pulut hitam kueh/cake recipes and are not derived from a direct laboratory analysis of the Sinpopo Coffee product. 2. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on black glutinous rice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite cake product. gi_category is 'low' per the Veritas threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low). 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for estimation. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for estimation. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for estimation. 6. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for estimation. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for estimation. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for estimation. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used for estimation. 10. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as black glutinous rice cake contains no significant vitamin C; this is confirmed by the absence of vitamin-C-containing ingredients in the recipe. 11. The food is classified as Singaporean cuisine based on the traditional Malay/Singaporean origin of pulut hitam desserts, notwithstanding the modern café-style presentation by Sinpopo Coffee. 12. Allergen profile includes eggs, gluten (wheat flour), and coconut (tree nut); consumers with relevant allergies should exercise caution. 13. Price data from field survey data (SGD 12.35 per slice) is preserved as contextual metadata but is not used in nutritional calculations.
